rice.name: Rice (चावल)
rice.soil: Clayey loam, alluvial soil. pH 5.5-7.0. Water-retentive.
rice.water_note: Requires standing water during most growth stages. 1200-1800mm rainfall needed.
tips: Use certified seeds — 40-50 kg/hectare for direct seeding
Maintain 2-3 seedlings per hill for transplanted rice
Apply nitrogen in 3 splits: basal, tillering, panicle initiation
Drain field 10-15 days before harvest for grain drying
For SRI method: transplant single seedlings at 8-12 days, wider spacing

wheat.name: Wheat (गेहूं)
wheat.soil: Loam to clay loam. Well-drained. pH 6.0-7.5.
wheat.water_note: 4-6 irrigations needed. Critical at CRI, tillering, flowering stages.
wheat.temperature: 20-25°C during growing season. Cool during grain filling.
tips: Sow timely — each week delay after Nov 15 loses ~50 kg/ha yield
Seed rate: 100 kg/ha for normal, 125 kg/ha for late sowing
First irrigation 21 days after sowing (CRI stage) is critical
Apply 1/3 N + full P&K as basal. Remaining N in 2 splits.
Spray Mancozeb if rust spots appear on leaves

cotton.name: Cotton (कपास)
cotton.soil: Black soil (regur), alluvial soil. Well-drained. pH 6.0-8.0.
cotton.water_note: 600-1000mm. Avoid waterlogging. Drip irrigation saves 40-50% water.
tips: Maintain 90x45 cm spacing for Bt hybrids
Apply 120:60:60 NPK/ha in black soil regions
Intercrop with short-duration pulses for extra income
Pick cotton in morning when bolls are fully open
Remove and destroy pink bollworm infested bolls

sugarcane.name: Sugarcane (गन्ना)
tips: Use 3-budded setts from disease-free seed cane
Apply 275:115:115 NPK/ha for high-yielding varieties
Ratoon management: remove dead stalks, apply fertilizer early
Trash mulching conserves moisture and adds organic matter
Get sugarcane slip/weight receipt at the mill for payment

maize.name: Maize (मक्का)
maize.soil: Well-drained loam to sandy loam. pH 5.8-8.0.
maize.water_note: 500-800mm. Critical stages: tasseling and grain filling.
maize.temperature: 25-30°C. Frost-sensitive. Needs warm soil for germination.
tips: Seed rate: 20-25 kg/ha. Row spacing: 60x20 cm
Apply 120:60:40 NPK/ha. Full P&K + 1/3 N as basal
Earthing up at 30-35 days prevents lodging
Harvest when husks are dry and grains are hard
Dry grains to 12-14% moisture for safe storage

mustard.name: Mustard (सरसों)
mustard.soil: Sandy loam to clay loam. Well-drained. pH 6.0-7.5.
tips: Seed rate: 5-6 kg/ha. Row spacing: 30 cm
Irrigate at sowing, branching, and pod formation stages
Aphid is the major pest — spray Dimethoate if infestation >5/plant
Harvest when lower pods turn brown and start shattering
Dry on tarpaulin, not bare ground, to maintain oil quality

tomato.name: Tomato (टमाटर)
seasons: Kharif: Jun-Jul, Rabi: Oct-Nov, Spring: Jan-Feb
tomato.soil: Well-drained sandy loam to clay. pH 6.0-7.0. Rich in organic matter.
tomato.temperature: 20-30°C. Doesn't set fruit above 40°C or below 10°C.
tips: Transplant 25-30 day old seedlings
Staking increases yield by 20-30% and reduces disease
Apply Calcium spray to prevent blossom end rot
Harvest at breaker stage for long-distance transport
Crop rotation with non-solanaceous crops prevents wilt

potato.name: Potato (आलू)
tips: Use 40-50 g seed tubers. Cut large tubers keeping 2-3 eyes each
Seed rate: 25-30 quintals/ha
Earthing up twice — at 25 and 45 days after planting
Sprout inhibitor (Isopropyl N-chlorophenoxy acetate) for stored potatoes
Store in cool, dark, ventilated place. Check weekly for rot.

onion.name: Onion (प्याज)
onion.soil: Sandy loam to medium black soil. Well-drained. pH 6.0-7.5.
tips: Transplant 6-8 week old seedlings. 15x10 cm spacing.
Reduce irrigation 15 days before harvest for better storage
Cure bulbs in shade for 4-5 days after harvest
Store in well-ventilated, dry place on raised platforms
Nasik-type onions store 4-6 months. Bellary-type: 2-3 months.
